projectSummary: A renewable energy research project with three parallel roles (Alice – literature collection, Bob – simulations, Charlie – analysis) culminating in a final paper submission on November 5. The simulation software license expires next month, so the source document explicitly recommends renewing it before any experiments begin; license renewal is therefore the first critical-path action and a precondition for Bob's simulation work and, downstream, Charlie's analysis and the final submission.
objectives: Collect the underlying literature on renewable energy (Alice)
Submit the final research paper by November 5

tasks.name: Renew the simulation software license before starting experiments

risks.name: Simulation software license expires next month
risks.description: Simulation software license expires next month
risks.solution: Renew the simulation software license before any experiments begin (per the source document's recommendation); assign a named owner for the renewal, verify access is restored before Bob starts simulations, and confirm the renewal is in place before the literature → simulation → analysis chain begins so the November 5 paper submission is not at risk.

recommendations.title: Renew the simulation software license before starting experiments (verbatim from the source document).
recommendations.description: Renew the simulation software license before starting experiments (verbatim from the source document).

recommendations.title: Sequence the work in the documented order — literature → license renewal → simulations → analysis → final paper — so the November 5 submission date is protected.
recommendations.description: Sequence the work in the documented order — literature → license renewal → simulations → analysis → final paper — so the November 5 submission date is protected.

recommendations.title: Confirm license renewal is complete (and a named owner is recorded) before Bob begins simulations, so the dependency is visible rather than implicit.
recommendations.description: Confirm license renewal is complete (and a named owner is recorded) before Bob begins simulations, so the dependency is visible rather than implicit.
